title: 11 Herbal Creams For Jaundice
intro_desc: Herbal creams for jaundice can be a natural and effective way to relieve symptoms and promote healing. Jaundice is a condition characterized by a yellowing of the skin and eyes, often caused by liver problems or bile duct blockages. Some herbal teas, like those made from Silybum marianum, also known as milk thistle, contain silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that can help protect the liver and promote its function.  Curcuma longa, or turmeric, is another herb that has been used for centuries to treat liver and digestive issues. It contains a compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce liver inflammation and promote bile production.  Another herb, Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, has been traditionally used to treat liver and digestive problems, including jaundice. It contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help protect the liver and promote its function.  Using herbal teas to relieve jaundice can bring several benefits to your life. For one, it can help reduce symptoms like yellowing of the skin and eyes, and can also promote healing and recovery. Additionally, herbal teas can be a natural and non-invasive way to treat jaundice, without the risks of side effects associated with prescription medications. By using herbal teas, you can also take control of your health and well-being, and make informed decisions about your treatment options.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams contains silymarin, a mixture of bioactive compounds including silibinin, silidianin, and silicristin. These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce liver damage and promote liver cell regeneration. Silymarin also has a protective effect on liver cells by blocking the action of toxins and free radicals. The antioxidant properties of silymarin help to reduce the levels of bilirubin in the blood, which is a key factor in the development of jaundice. By reducing liver damage and promoting liver cell regeneration, silymarin in Silybum marianum creams can help to alleviate the symptoms of jaundice.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal creams for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum flowers and 2 cups of coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water until it reaches 120°F to 140°F.
Add the dried Silybum marianum flowers to the coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Let the mixture cool, then whip it with an electric mixer until it becomes a smooth cream. Store it in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin as its active constituents. These compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function. The curcuminoids also have a strong ability to scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the liver, which is beneficial for treating jaundice. Additionally, the antiproliferative and antifibrotic properties of the curcuminoids can help prevent liver scarring and promote healthy liver regeneration. By using Curcuma longa creams, patients with jaundice may experience improved liver function and a reduction in symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Curcuma longa powder, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the Curcuma longa powder to the melted mixture and stir well.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly, then add the vitamin E oil and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify completely before applying it to the affected area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, along with flavonoids and phenolic acids. Glycyrrhizin has hepatoprotective properties, which help to protect the liver from damage. The flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams have antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver. This can be beneficial for people with jaundice, a condition characterized by a buildup of bilirubin in the blood, which can cause liver damage. The anti-inflammatory properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may also help to reduce liver inflammation and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Grind the dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
In a saucepan, heat the coconut oil and beeswax over low heat, stirring until the beeswax is melted.
Add the ground Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and vitamin E oil to the saucepan, stirring well.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool. Once cooled, pour the mixture into a container and store it in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cynara scolymus creams contains active constituents such as flavonoids, saponins, and sesquiterpenes. These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce liver damage and promote liver health. The antioxidants in Cynara scolymus creams, like quercetin and kaempferol, help to neutralize free radicals that can cause liver cells to die. The anti-inflammatory compounds, including cynarin and inulin, help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the liver, which can aid in the treatment of jaundice. The saponins in Cynara scolymus creams also have a choleretic effect, which means they help to increase bile production and flow, which can help to remove bilirubin from the body and reduce jaundice symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Cynara scolymus herbal creams for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cynara scolymus flowers (artichoke flowers), 1 cup of coconut oil, and a double boiler.
Dry the artichoke flowers in a low oven (150°F) for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Combine the dried artichoke flowers and coconut oil in the double boiler. Heat for 2 hours, then let cool.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, pressing on the solids to get the oil. Discard the solids.
Store the Cynara scolymus cream in a cool, dark place. Apply topically to affected skin 2-3 times a day to help treat jaundice.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains a compound called zingerone, which has anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce the swelling associated with jaundice. Zingiber officinale creams also contain a compound called gingerol, which has antioxidant properties that help protect the liver from damage. The antioxidant properties of gingerol can help reduce the levels of bilirubin in the blood, which is a major symptom of jaundice. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of zingerone and gingerol in Zingiber officinale creams may help alleviate the symptoms of jaundice.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup coconut oil, 1/4 cup beeswax, 1/2 cup shea butter, 2 tablespoons Zingiber officinale powder, 2 tablespoons honey.
Melt coconut oil, beeswax, and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over boiling water.
Remove melted mixture from heat and stir in Zingiber officinale powder and honey until well combined.
Pour mixture into a silicone mold or a glass jar and let it cool and solidify completely.
Once solid, remove from mold or jar and store in an airtight container in a cool place. Use 1-2 teaspoons as needed to help alleviate jaundice symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: I couldn't find any reliable information that suggests Ginkgo biloba creams are good for jaundice. However, I can provide some general information about Ginkgo biloba. Ginkgo biloba creams contain flavonoids, bilobalide and ginkgolides as active constituents.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly before adding Ginkgo biloba extract and vitamin E oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and harden completely before use. Apply a small amount to the affected area as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale creams contains flavonoids, such as taraxasterol and taraxol, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with jaundice. Taraxasterol, in particular, has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, meaning it can help protect the liver from damage. Additionally, the sesquiterpenes present in Taraxacum officinale creams have been found to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune system's response to liver injury. By reducing liver inflammation and oxidative stress, Taraxacum officinale creams may help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Taraxacum officinale herbal creams for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Taraxacum officinale flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/2 cup of beeswax.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add the fresh Taraxacum officinale flowers to the melted mixture and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the flowers. Let it cool until it reaches 100°F to 110°F.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Once set, your Taraxacum officinale cream is ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica creams contains triterpenoid saponins, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The triterpenoid saponins, specifically asiaticoside and madecassoside, help to improve liver function and reduce bilirubin levels, which is beneficial for jaundice patients. The glycosides in Centella asiatica creams also have a protective effect on the liver cells, preventing damage and promoting regeneration. Additionally, the flavonoids present in Centella asiatica creams have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce liver inflammation and promote a healthy liver environment. By improving liver function and reducing inflammation, Centella asiatica creams can help to alleviate jaundice symptoms and promote liver health.
remedies.image_alt: Centella asiatica herbal creams for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Centella asiatica leaves, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Dry the Centella asiatica leaves in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2-3 hours. Let them cool.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add the dried Centella asiatica leaves, shea butter, and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, the Centella asiatica cream is ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains bioactive constituents such as alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with jaundice. The polyphenols in Echinacea purpurea creams have been shown to have a protective effect on liver cells, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of jaundice. The flavonoids and sesquiterpenes in Echinacea purpurea creams have also been found to have antiviral and immunomodulatory effects, which can help to prevent the progression of jaundice. By reducing inflammation and protecting liver cells, Echinacea purpurea creams can help to alleviate the symptoms of jaundice and promote liver health.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea root, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Combine distilled water and dried Echinacea purpurea root in a saucepan. Heat on low until the mixture boils. Let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the Echinacea purpurea root. Let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
In a separate bowl, mix together beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter. Heat the mixture in a double boiler or in the microwave in 10-second increments until it's smooth.
Combine the cooled Echinacea purpurea mixture with the melted beeswax mixture. Stir well and pour the mixture into a container. Let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains carnosic acid and camphor as its active constituents. These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and promote liver cell regeneration. The antioxidant activity of carnosic acid can neutralize free radicals, which can damage liver cells and contribute to jaundice. Camphor's anti-inflammatory properties can also help reduce liver inflammation and promote liver function. By reducing inflammation and promoting liver cell regeneration, Rosmarinus officinalis creams may help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of rosemary essential oil (Rosmarinus officinalis), and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, remove the bowl from the heat and stir in the rosemary essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Let the mixture cool and thicken. This may take about 30 minutes, depending on the temperature.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool completely before applying it to the skin to help with jaundice symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium creams contains active constituents like thujone, borneol, and sesquiterpenes. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help reduce swelling and infection in the liver, which is often affected in jaundice. The bitter compounds in Artemisia absinthium, such as absinthin, can also stimulate digestion and liver function, promoting the elimination of bile and bilirubin. Additionally, the sesquiterpenes in Artemisia absinthium creams have been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the liver cells from damage and oxidative stress. By promoting liver function and reducing inflammation, Artemisia absinthium creams may help alleviate the symptoms of jaundice.
remedies.image_alt: Artemisia absinthium herbal creams for jaundice
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers, and 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil)
Heat the carrier oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, until it reaches 120-140°F (49-60°C)
Add the dried Artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers to the heated oil, and let it steep for 2-3 hours, or overnight
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a small saucepan, heating gently until the mixture is smooth and creamy

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent jaundice in newborns. Certain herbal teas, such as milk thistle, contain compounds that support liver health. These compounds can aid in the removal of bilirubin, a pigment that causes jaundice. Regular consumption may help reduce the risk of jaundice, but its effectiveness varies depending on individual factors.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for jaundice can be a helpful addition to your treatment plan, but it's essential to use them carefully. Drinking them every day may not be safe, as some herbs can cause liver problems or interact with medications. It's best to follow the recommended amount and consult the packaging for guidance.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas for jaundice can start showing results within a few days to a week. Some people notice an improvement in their condition after drinking the teas daily. The body's response can vary, but most people see noticeable changes within 7-10 days of consistent use. This timeframe can help the liver recover and reduce bilirubin levels.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for jaundice is best in the morning. This allows the body to digest and process the tea's ingredients throughout the day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, including those for jaundice. Some herbs, like dandelion and milk thistle, can affect the way the liver processes medications. This can lead to increased or decreased levels of medication in the body. If you're taking prescription medications for jaundice and drinking herbal teas, monitor your condition closely and report any changes to your healthcare provider.
faq_pregnant: It's generally not a good idea to drink herbal teas for jaundice when pregnant. Some herbs can cause problems or interact with other things in a way that might affect the baby. It's always best to be cautious with new foods or drinks, especially during pregnancy. It's best to stick with what's recommended or trusted.
faq_breastfeeding: It's best to avoid herbal teas for jaundice while breastfeeding. Some herbs can pass into breast milk and affect the baby. Jaundice can be a serious issue, and it's essential to find natural treatments that are safe. If you're considering herbal teas, make sure to do thorough research and understand their potential impact on your baby.
title_2: 11 Herbal Teas and Creams for Liver Disease Patients with Jaundice
